entries.message:
<@U9HDNS9S4> <@U03J8UXUDPF> <@U08EVBTE69E>

Hey guys! We're trying to automate how our SMM works — meaning, ideally we'd like to generate posts about new product releases based on what is being posted to this channel.

Can I ask you to post comments in threads to the features that deserve being highlighted in our marketing? Like these:
• <https://adapty-team.slack.com/archives/C04SY70QZ0T/p1772010847453799?thread_ts=1771941058.884159&cid=C04SY70QZ0T>
• <https://adapty-team.slack.com/archives/C04SY70QZ0T/p1770977999507989?thread_ts=1770819397.356349&cid=C04SY70QZ0T>
• <https://adapty-team.slack.com/archives/C04SY70QZ0T/p1769776849011559?thread_ts=1769766798.436629&cid=C04SY70QZ0T>
• <https://adapty-team.slack.com/archives/C04SY70QZ0T/p1768803495924489>
We can then take this info and produce posts based on this. Thank you!

thread.text:

Hey guys! We're trying to automate how our SMM works — meaning, ideally we'd like to generate posts about new product releases based on what is being posted to this channel.

Can I ask you to post comments in threads to the features that deserve being highlighted in our marketing? Like these:

• <https://adapty-team.slack.com/archives/C04SY70QZ0T/p1768803495924489>
We can then take this info and produce posts based on this. Thank you!
what comments?
> Like these
Describing how the features work and their value — what should be highlighted in marketing

Hey, I just saw this and had an idea about creating a workflow for analyzing the codebase and MRs, since some features are being released in complete silence, and now we also have L3 resources available.

Ideally, each MR should include a product description of the feature, so Claude (and anyone else) can read it or simply copy-paste it when needed.

Just my passing thoughts :slightly_smiling_face:
hey! i do it everytime on cool features and plan to do it further!

entries.name: Price import/export interface
entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5120|Price import/export interface>
Release date: Feb 24, 2026
Affected areas: Products

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5120|Price import/export interface>
Release date: Feb 24, 2026
Affected areas: Products
<@U08ASB4GYR4> notification
link to epic: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4651|ADP-4651>
Will the price on the dashboard in the placeholder be updated?

yeah, it should, for my knowledge updating prices takes some time, <@U08ASB4GYR4> can correct me If I am wrong

if it is not updated, then you can ping me and I will look into it

This price not updated. It's only used when creating a product in Adapty.The price synchronization we implemented as part of this feature applies to a downloadable price file that the user can view and edit. I think that at some point in the future we will either update this value according to the data or abandon it in favor of geo-pricing.

<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> FYI new feature release

Now clients *can set different prices for different regions right in the Adapty dashboard*, and these *country-specific prices* will be applied to your products in App Store Connect and/or Google Play Console automatically. <https://adapty.io/docs/edit-product>

entries.content:
[Denis Dolbilin]: Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5120|Price import/export interface>
Release date: Feb 24, 2026
Affected areas: Products

[Denis Dolbilin]: <@U08ASB4GYR4> notification

[Denis Dolbilin]: link to epic: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4651|ADP-4651>

[Vitaly Kuzmin]: Will the price on the dashboard in the placeholder be updated?

[Denis Dolbilin]: yeah, it should, for my knowledge updating prices takes some time, <@U08ASB4GYR4> can correct me If I am wrong

if it is not updated, then you can ping me and I will look into it

[Roman Miasnikov]: This price not updated. It's only used when creating a product in Adapty.The price synchronization we implemented as part of this feature applies to a downloadable price file that the user can view and edit. I think that at some point in the future we will either update this value according to the data or abandon it in favor of geo-pricing.

[Sofia Pikuleva]: <!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> FYI new feature release

Now clients *can set different prices for different regions right in the Adapty dashboard*, and these *country-specific prices* will be applied to your products in App Store Connect and/or Google Play Console automatically. <https://adapty.io/docs/edit-product>

entries.name: Provide market insights for top countries
entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5347|Provide market insights for top countries>
Release date: Feb 11, 2026
Dependencies: None
Effected areas: Autopilot

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-5347|Provide market insights for top countries>
Release date: Feb 11, 2026
Dependencies: None
Effected areas: Autopilot

hey team!
sharing a cool Autopilot update here, which was requested by many

*now you get competitors&benchmarks data not only about the US, but about your top-5 generating countries*

you can do crazy things:
• see how your competitors are priced in let's say Japan, and what's their strategy here
• get conversion rates benchmarks not overall (as it was before), but country based. now as a VPN app onwer you can see "how VPN apps convert to trial in Germany"
fyi - the recommendation flow and the algorithm remains the same, it is still based on the US prices as a core flow (will change it later)

i think it's a great angle for big apps, to get a new source of information
attaching a short video with verbal description
<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> <!subteam^S03KC9S1QMC> <!subteam^S02U0UTPEQM>

entries.name: Show Geo Pricing Opportunities with basic recommendations
entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4887|Show Geo Pricing Opportunities with basic recommendations>
Release date: Jan 30, 2026
Affected areas: *Autopilot*

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4887|Show Geo Pricing Opportunities with basic recommendations>
Release date: Jan 30, 2026
Affected areas: *Autopilot*

hey team!
we've released a new feature - geo-pricing recommendations! :earth_africa:

Autopilot now chooses the top-5 countries by revenue, and then provides pricing signals for this countries based on conversion rate comprising and Adapty Pricing index .

*for example*: 1) the conversion in Germany is _twice better than average_ (4% vs 2%); 2) and pricing index tells that purchasing power is _high_. it means that it's a great idea for you to try and _increase_ the price in Germany

so the personalised list of A/B tests remains based on US market (as a core recommendation flow for paywall improvement), but the signals are targeting the other countries

*check the video to get more context!* 
<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> <!subteam^S03KC9S1QMC> <!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> fyi

what comes next:
• deeper country analytics 
    ◦ get analysis of the competitors prices in these top countries 
    ◦ see the paywall CR benchmarks by country (what is average trial-paid conversion  for VPN apps in Germany)
• opportunity to update product prices in these countries within adapty (integration with v2. of product export)
<@U08SQQGK1A7>, <@U02GSCVUPHU> please add this to your content plans :pray:
Hi <@U08EVBTE69E> so the current 2.28% we see there, is it global CR average of the category of our selected app, right?
2,28% is this app's CR, not average of the industry!

entries.name: Ads Manager product update (Ads+CPP, Charts, Create Adapty segments from Ads Manager, set bids to Avg CPT / CPA)
entries.message:
Feature: Ads Manager product update (Ads+CPP, Charts, Create Adapty segments from Ads Manager, set bids to Avg CPT / CPA)
Release date: Mon 19, 2026
Effected areas: Ads Manager, Analytics

thread.text:
Feature: Ads Manager product update (Ads+CPP, Charts, Create Adapty segments from Ads Manager, set bids to Avg CPT / CPA)
Release date: Mon 19, 2026
Effected areas: Ads Manager, Analytics

1. Custom Ads (CPP per Ad Group)
You can now create ads directly in Ads Manager and assign different Custom Product Pages per ad group. This solves the problem of having to switch tools or do manual setup in Apple Ads to map CPPs to specific ad groups and intents.

2. Ad-Level Analytics
We added performance analytics at the ad level. This eliminates the creative/CPP blind spot and makes it clear which ad setup is driving results.

3. Charts on Campaigns / Ad Groups / Keywords
We introduced interactive charts across campaigns, ad groups, and keywords, with support for multiple metrics on one chart. This solves slow trend analysis and makes it easier to spot correlations and changes over time.

4. Open a Keyword Chart from the Keywords Table
 You can now open a chart for any keyword directly from the keywords table. This enables precise, day-by-day performance analysis for each individual keyword without extra navigation.

5. Create Segments for Paywalls/AB test from Ads Manager!
You can select campaigns, ad groups, and keywords and create an Adapty segment in a couple of clicks—right from Ads Manager. This solves the gap between acquisition structure and paywall personalization, enabling tailored paywalls much faster.

6. New Bulk Editing Actions: Set Bids to Avg CPT / CPA (with Coefficient)
You can now quickly set bids in bulk based on CPT/CPA using a coefficient — for example, set bids to 90% of avg CPT(Cost per Tap) when performance is below expectations, or 110% when it’s outperforming. This removes repetitive manual recalibration and helps apply consistent bid logic at scale.
(currently for manual changes; automation support is coming later)

entries.name: Hello everyone! :wave::santa:
entries.message:
Hello everyone! :wave::santa:
Christmas is no reason to stop the migration to the new tech domain :globe_with_meridians: — and it’s even a chance to make the Django admin addresses clearer :wrench:
New addresses from now: :pushpin:
• LMS: <https://lms.adpinfra.dev|lms.adpinfra.dev> (Adapty Knowledge Portal)
• SuperBI: <https://superbi.adpinfra.dev|superbi.adpinfra.dev>
New addresses from ~5th of January~ *now*::pushpin:
• Django Admin: <https://django-admin.adapty.io/admin/login/?next=/admin/|django-admin.adapty.io/admin/login/?next=/admin/> (Previously <https://api-admin.adapty.io/admin/login/?next=/admin/>)
• Django Admin EU: <https://django-admin-eu.adapty.io/admin/login/?next=/admin/|django-admin-eu.adapty.io/admin/login/?next=/admin/> (Previously <https://api-eu-admin.adapty.io/admin/login/?next=/admin/>)
As always, please:
• Update bookmarks / saved links :star:
• If you use integrations or scripts — check and, if needed, replace the domain :gear:
• If you have access problems — reply in the thread or message me directly :sparkles:

entries.name: *Feature:* Refactored Profile context
entries.message:
*Feature:* Refactored Profile context
*Release date:* Dec 23, 2025
*Additional info:*
• <https://gitlab.adapty.io/adapty/adapty-dashboard-api/-/merge_requests/11386|Merge Request>
*Note*:
I slightly refactored the profile creation flow and the Customer User ID assignment. There were no changes to the logic, so everything should remain the same for clients.
*Affected areas:*
• requests from SDK and Server-Side API;
• analytics (Installs).

thread.text:

*Release date:* Dec 23, 2025
*Additional info:*
• <https://gitlab.adapty.io/adapty/adapty-dashboard-api/-/merge_requests/11386|Merge Request>
*Note*:
I slightly refactored the profile creation flow and the Customer User ID assignment. There were no changes to the logic, so everything should remain the same for clients.
*Affected areas:*
• requests from SDK and Server-Side API;
• analytics (Installs).
<!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> fyi

entries.name: Fix resetting builder product style during product replacing
entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4126|Fix resetting builder product style during product replacing>
Release date: Dec 15, 2025
Effected areas: Paywall Builder

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4126|Fix resetting builder product style during product replacing>
Release date: Dec 15, 2025
Effected areas: Paywall Builder

hey team!

<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ>
<!subteam^S03KM96DV0U>

this one is a blast! now when users duplicate their paywalls the style/design of product section remains the same. this was a huge pain for many clients, because the design was being reset before, and it slowed them down when making lots of paywalls and experiments

i've heard about it at least 10 times, and I think many will be happy with this fix. let's share some great christmas updates for them
<@U08MPLZ50NP> <@U075H1SMAJC> also may be valuable for you
finally

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4490|Search apps by name>
Release date: Dec 11th 2025
Affected areas: top navigation bar
:clap::clap::clap:

<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> <!subteam^S03KM96DV0U>

Now users can search apps by name
Now way, this is finally happening! )))) :tada::clap::skin-tone-3:
Well-awaited release:heart: Thank you!

Hi team, why don’t some accounts have this feature enabled?
Is there a threshold for the number of apps after which search becomes available (for example, starting from 10 apps per account)?
Here’s an example from an account with 5 apps.
<@U07FKBD6QDS> Hi! search input appears if there are more than 7 apps

entries.name: Fix number of views in Onboardings
entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/ADP/boards/180?quickFilter=269&selectedIssue=ADP-5034|Fix number of views in Onboardings>
Release date: Dec 9th 2025
Affected areas: *Analytics, Onboardings*

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/ADP/boards/180?quickFilter=269&selectedIssue=ADP-5034|Fix number of views in Onboardings>
Release date: Dec 9th 2025
Affected areas: *Analytics, Onboardings*
Views were not counted when the user left after the first page. Now they are counted.

<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> Hey!

We had a bug related to the number of onboarding views.

If an onboarding contained only one screen, we didn’t count its views.

Now we’ve fixed it.

entries.content:
[Dmitry Bastrikin]: Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/ADP/boards/180?quickFilter=269&selectedIssue=ADP-5034|Fix number of views in Onboardings>
Release date: Dec 9th 2025
Affected areas: *Analytics, Onboardings*

[Dmitry Bastrikin]: Views were not counted when the user left after the first page. Now they are counted.

[Artem Frolov]: <!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> Hey!

We had a bug related to the number of onboarding views.

If an onboarding contained only one screen, we didn’t count its views.

Now we’ve fixed it.

entries.name: new template library
entries.message:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4278|new template library>
Release date: Dec 2, 2025
Effected areas: Paywall Builder

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4278|new template library>
Release date: Dec 2, 2025
Effected areas: Paywall Builder

<!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> <!subteam^S086DHAHL8J> <!subteam^S03KC9S1QMC>

Now users can filter paywalls using:
• Number of products: 1 product, More than 1 product
• Image and video: All, With image or video, No media
• Components: Free trial toggle, Trial timeline, Reviews, Timer
• Length: All, Short, Long
• Background theme: All, Light, Dark
We’ve also highlighted popular and seasonal paywalls to make them easier to find.

Hey folks! Mainly <@U09HGKVGSTV>
Some of the new templates seem odd.
Pic 1 - old template. Product cards display the actual product duration selected on the paywall. Paywall makes sense as is without editing (which is important for demo).
Pic 2 - new template. Same set of product selected, but the template displays random duration and even more random prices. Looks wacky and raises eyebrows.
(I know that the price tags in preview are not supposed to be correct, but they should definitively make more sense than this)

The reason seems to be that the old templates use tags for product cards, which is the correct way to design the cards (pic 3) while the new templates just have "hard" text.

Hey, thanks for raising the problem

We will fix it

entries.content:
[German Shteynardt]: Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4278|new template library>
Release date: Dec 2, 2025
Effected areas: Paywall Builder

[Artem Frolov]: <!subteam^S03KM96DV0U> <!subteam^S086DHAHL8J> <!subteam^S03KC9S1QMC>

Now users can filter paywalls using:
• Number of products: 1 product, More than 1 product
• Image and video: All, With image or video, No media
• Components: Free trial toggle, Trial timeline, Reviews, Timer
• Length: All, Short, Long
• Background theme: All, Light, Dark
We’ve also highlighted popular and seasonal paywalls to make them easier to find.

[Artem Rukavishnikov]: Hey folks! Mainly <@U09HGKVGSTV>
Some of the new templates seem odd.
Pic 1 - old template. Product cards display the actual product duration selected on the paywall. Paywall makes sense as is without editing (which is important for demo).
Pic 2 - new template. Same set of product selected, but the template displays random duration and even more random prices. Looks wacky and raises eyebrows.
(I know that the price tags in preview are not supposed to be correct, but they should definitively make more sense than this)

The reason seems to be that the old templates use tags for product cards, which is the correct way to design the cards (pic 3) while the new templates just have "hard" text.

[Artem Frolov]: Hey, thanks for raising the problem

We will fix it

thread.text:
Feature: <https://adaptyio.atlassian.net/browse/ADP-4487|Developer role in Adapty>
Release date: Nov 25, 2025
Effected areas: for this new role
• Removed all metrics for this role
• Removed sections
    ◦ overview
    ◦ analytics
    ◦ refund saver
    ◦ ua
    ◦ asa
Side note: if a user was previously assigned other role, enabled email notifications with financial summaries, and then was turned into developer, they will no longer receive such emails

<!subteam^S07BZPQDBUJ> many customers requested it. it's now possible to add developers to projects without giving them access to metrics, so they can configure everything, but they can't see revenue and other metrics
<https://adapty.io/docs/members-settings>
Great update! Thank you, team!
